CREATE TABLE events (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, event_time TIMESTAMP DEFAULT now());
SELECT id, event_time FROM events WHERE event_time >= now() - INTERVAL '1 hour';
CREATE TABLE users (user_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, username VARCHAR(50));
CREATE TABLE user_events (user_id INTEGER, event_id INTEGER, FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(user_id), FOREIGN KEY (event_id) REFERENCES events(id));

SELECT u.username, COUNT(ue.event_id) AS event_count FROM users u JOIN user_events ue ON u.user_id = ue.user_id WHERE ue.event_id IN (SELECT id FROM events WHERE event_time >= now()) GROUP BY u.username;
SELECT now() AS current_time, (SELECT MAX(event_time) FROM events) AS last_event_time;

SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE event_time BETWEEN now() - INTERVAL '1 day' AND now()) AS events_today, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE event_time < now() - INTERVAL '1 day') AS past_events;
WITH TimeCheck AS (SELECT now() AS current_time, (SELECT MIN(event_time) FROM events) AS first_event_time) SELECT current_time, first_event_time FROM TimeCheck;
SELECT now() AS current_time, UNIX_TIMESTAMP(now()) AS current_unix_timestamp;
SELECT now() + INTERVAL '1 hour' AS future_time;
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW recent_events AS SELECT * FROM events WHERE event_time >= now() - INTERVAL '30 minutes';
